text: The integration of AI and autonomous agents in crypto trading is advancing, with platforms like RoboNetHQ and Donut AI launching prompt-to-quant execution engines and proactive AI trading agents that can build, backtest, and deploy trading strategies autonomously on venues including Hyperliquid, Lighter, and Polymarket. These tools aim to reduce emotional trading and improve systematic execution.
author: elfa_2039071869232726314

text: Significant advancements in quantum computing and cryptography have been announced, with two breakthrough papers improving Shor's algorithm to crack RSA and elliptic curve cryptography more efficiently. Google Quantum AI and startup Oratomic have optimized separate layers of the quantum stack, increasing the feasibility of quantum attacks on Bitcoin and Ethereum signatures. This has increased confidence in the arrival of a cryptographically relevant quantum computer (q-day) by 2032, prompting a narrative shift towards post-quantum cryptography and increased R&D efforts.
author: elfa_2039065688644428260

text: The Subprime AI Crisis Is Here AI boom looks big partly because everyone in the chain is hiding or absorbing real costs, and that illusion starts breaking once the subsidies run out. TLDR • AI gets adopted on fake-cheap economics (real costs hidden by subsidies and VC cash) • Everyone in the chain builds on that illusion (loads of debts taken) • Users develop unsustainable habits but costs keep rising underneath • The crisis starts when someone needs real margins (when AI labs can't just keep subsidizing usage) • Labs push prices up or access down —> Startups get hit and raise prices/ cut features —> Users get angry and churn • Illusion of value breaks with demand collapsing + startup failures + huge debts

text: Core Foundation has announced a partnership with Z Protocol, marking its first "franchise protocol." Z Protocol is positioned as an EVM-compatible Layer 1 built for Zcash to expand application-layer capabilities and bring more onchain use cases. It will adopt Core's "Satoshi Plus" consensus mechanism, allowing both Zcash stakers and PoW miners to participate in network security and validation. text: Token issuance failure rates have reached record levels.At the EthCC conference, 21Shares researcher Darius Moukhtarzade stated that the core issue lies in the "low circulating supply, high fully diluted valuation (FDV)" model prevalent in 2024-2025: an initially small circulating supply drives up the price, followed by a concentrated release of internal unlocking pressure, creating selling pressure that retail investors cannot absorb, leading to a downward "death spiral." He proposed a sustainable token economy framework for 2026: increasing the initial circulating supply to over 20% to improve price discovery and reduce the impact of subsequent unlocking; requiring a clear PMF and a real user growth/revenue model before token issuance; and strengthening value capture mechanisms. — link

text: Microeconomics in Public Policy: A Practitioner's View Singapore's core argument is that governments should not fight market forces when solving public problems. Instead, they should use prices, incentives, competition, and economic logic to design policies better • Use market principles when implementing policy • Price scarce resources instead of allocating them by bureaucracy • Give help through cash or cash-like support rather than broad subsidies or in-kind benefits
author: admin#1742094092

text: Google Quantum AI released a whitepaper warning that cracking 256-bit ECC, widely used in crypto wallets, requires fewer resources than expected. With under 500k physical qubits, it could be cracked in minutes. Google urged the industry to accelerate its migration to Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC). Dragonfly Managing Partner Haseeb noted this research improves ECDSA cracking efficiency by ~20x, pushing the expected PQC migration timeline forward to around 2029. He highlighted Google's high caution, as they verified the results using ZK proofs without revealing the actual quantum circuits. — link

text: Google Quantum AI - Safeguarding cryptocurrency by disclosing quantum vulnerabilities responsibly Quantum risk to crypto is more immediate than most people think. Future quantum computers may need far fewer resources than older estimates to break the elliptic-curve cryptography used by chains like Bitcoin and Ethereum Bitcoin is mainly vulnerable when public keys are exposed or reused, while Ethereum has a broader attack surface because of accounts, admin keys, validators, and smart contracts. Dormant coins/assets are also a huge unsolved problem, because abandoned funds cannot simply migrate themselves to safer cryptography later.
